author: Compost Man Unity code: XY
msg: I'm doing one for ULOs in a  Facebook group. Similar idea to support my ULOs/promote licenses. I'm thinking that any UNO will be welcome to contribute  by answering questions etc and promote their own licenses in exchange. Thinking through the details now. Something like, you are welcome to add a link to your own licenses at the bottom of every useful comment. ' if you found this helpful, please consider leasing one of my licences @', sort of thing. I will promote my own licences at a 50/50 split. This would need to be the minimum to prevent the usual race to the. I'm happy to pick up licences both actively and passively from this if the group is active. Happy for other UNOs to benefit from the platform, particularly if we can pick up a critical mass. What do you think? I've also got a Facebook page which I will announce later today or tomorrow.

author: Quoia75
msg:
**❓Question UNOs:**


I purchased an extra node with the intention of transferring it to another person. That person is now saying they would like me to retain ownership of the node, but they want to have the ability to manage and distribute the node's licenses to ULOs.


Is this possible? (probably a silly question). If it is, can any of you describe how this would be achieved and tracked? Do any of you have a similar arrangement?


TY

author: Coffee Nodes
msg: why doesn't the other person just create a wallet and keep it

author: Coffee Nodes
msg: you can create another wallet and keep it separate but imo if it's not your node, it's better to not be your responsability

author: Gwailow
msg: Could you not just put this in a separate wallet to your nodes, and then provide him with the QR code to log in as UNO and manage the leases

author: Zolee
msg: You could keep it in a separate wallet for them and whenever they would need to log in you can forward them a picture of the QR code

author: Gwailow
msg: But question is what is in it for you with this arrangement?

author: Quoia75
msg: Not super crypto savvy. Started freaking out a bit when I described the transfer process and cold wallet storage. But they have a fairly large email list and I know they would do great with license distribution.

author: Quoia75
msg: Family member. Bought the node as a gift.✔️

author: Gwailow
msg: ok - just sounds like a ball ache from your perspective

author: Quoia75
msg: You do what you can for those you love.❤️

author: Quoia75
msg: Thank you, brilliant.

author: Quoia75
msg: Thank you - this is the way.🙏🙌

author: Gwailow
msg: Perhaps just teach them the basics of logging in as UNO and create the wallet for them. Tell them under no circumstances to do anything else with the wallet apart from logging in as UNO

author: FriendlyUrchin
msg: I have such an arrangement. I sent them QR code via email for them to login as UNO. They just keep logged in. For rewards withdrawal - management portal you must be physically present to login since you must connect the wallet. But once it's done they also stay logged-in in the management portal.

author: FriendlyUrchin
msg: I wish I could do that. They are now invested. Even when they know the risks in case it doesn't go well some aftertaste will always be present. CLI and S&R and we can leave the worry behind :)

author: Quoia75
msg:
With the QR Code login, is this only a temp login though? If they say, restart their computer, would I then need to regen a new QR code for them?

Maybe once public API becomes available there will be a more robust way to have a co-manager of a node.🙏

author: FriendlyUrchin
msg: Restarting does not influence it. I do it all the time. It keeps you logged in the next time you open the browser window with the manag. portal. Clearing cache would log you out I guess.

author: Darko
msg: This is not an assumption but a technical knowledge by people who know what they did with IP ranges. The same day they cut off VPN people they also cut off wide range of legal IP addresses. For example if there is more than 50 or 100 people using the same base station in one city or a neighborhood they see this as a same IP cluster and  they shut it down. PRobably by mistake

author: Kay | Will never DM you first
msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - CLI Mini Guide** 

A  quick guide to the new CLI opt-in task.

𝗢𝘃𝗲𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗲𝘄:
CLI tasks require an active Unity license
CLI is an opt-in task, and license operators must enable it
CLI calls are live
Calls are real and non-synthetic
CLI calls look like a regular incoming call
CLI works in every region
Tasks are carrier-agnostic
Any SIM or eSIM that can receive incoming calls is supported
Compatible with iOS, even if the “Unity connection” task shows offline 

𝗡𝗼𝘁𝗶𝗳𝗶𝗰𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝘀:
When a CLI call is received, users get a notification
Notifications include a push notification and an in-app pop-up

𝗠𝗶𝘀𝘀𝗲𝗱 𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗹𝘀**:**
It’s OK if you miss the call
You can report a CLI call up to 24 hours after it’s received

𝗦𝗜𝗠** **𝗯𝗮𝗹𝗮𝗻𝗰𝗲 / 𝗮𝗰𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗲:
If your SIM or eSIM can receive incoming calls, it can accept CLI tests
You usually do NOT need an active balance on your SIM/eSIM to receive a CLI call, but this depends on your carrier, and some carriers may require it

𝗡𝗲𝘁𝘄𝗼𝗿𝗸 𝗜𝗻𝗰𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗶𝘃𝗲𝘀:
Unity Points are distributed immediately after a valid CLI task is completed 

CLI – verifying telecom activity at scale.

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2020940460231995656

🤝 **Official Unity** **Resources**
[**Unity**](https://unitynodes.io/) | [**Unity Litepaper**](https://unitynodes.io/documents/litepaper.pdf) | [**QR Code Login**](https://uno.unitynodes.io/) | [**Manage**](https://manage.unitynodes.io/) | [**Telegram**](https://t.me/unitynodes) | [**X**](https://x.com/UnityNodesIO) | [**YouTube**](https://youtube.com/@UnityNodesIO) | [**Bug Report**](https://unitynodes.io/bug-report) | [**Verification Bot**](https://t.me/UnityNetworkVerificationBot)
